description: Ambitious post-10/10 sound-product ideas. These are visible product bets, not 1.0 release gates.

ideas.title: Agent-assisted producer loop take selection
ideas.musical_payoff: Riotbox can render several takes, reject weak ones, and suggest the next concrete sound-design move a producer would actually try.
replay_realtime_risk: take ranking must run offline or outside the realtime callback
selected take decisions need replayable source, policy, seed, and action lineage
evidence_needed: calibrated human label comparison across accepted and rejected takes
candidate-vs-baseline metrics that explain why the selected take wins
review pack showing the suggested next production move
ideas.promotion_condition: Promote only after the 10/10 demo bank contains enough human-pass and human-weak labels to calibrate take selection.
ideas.release_1_0_boundary: Non-blocking for 1.0; cannot replace human listening or demo-ready gates.

ideas.title: Source-to-performance memory
ideas.musical_payoff: Riotbox remembers which source traits, chop policies, drum treatments, bass gestures, and destructive transitions produced strong results.
replay_realtime_risk: memory lookup must not bypass Source Graph identity or Session replay
learned preferences must remain inspectable and removable
evidence_needed: source-trait index with artifact and verdict lineage
before/after renders showing better policy choices on similar sources
negative examples proving weak memories do not get promoted
ideas.promotion_condition: Promote only when source traits and human verdicts have a stable schema and enough labeled examples.
ideas.release_1_0_boundary: Non-blocking for 1.0; useful after the release gate can already choose strong source-aware policies.

ideas.title: Live resampling and self-abuse
ideas.musical_payoff: The player can capture Riotbox output, degrade it, retrigger it, and promote it into a new playable source during a set.
replay_realtime_risk: capture must not block realtime audio
new playable sources need deterministic lineage and restore behavior
destructive processing must stay bounded against clipping and runaway feedback
evidence_needed: capture-lineage session fixture
offline render proving deterministic replay of captured output
audio QA for clipping, silence, feedback, and fallback collapse
ideas.promotion_condition: Promote only when source capture is modeled as first-class Source Graph and Session state.
ideas.release_1_0_boundary: Non-blocking for 1.0; must not create a second persistence or replay truth.

ideas.title: Stage-impact macro controls
ideas.musical_payoff: Panic stop, pressure rise, dirty restore, break shred, bass punch, and source-mangle become reliable room-changing controls.
replay_realtime_risk: macros must compile to normal ActionCommand paths
timing, undo, and restore semantics must stay deterministic
macro work must not hide blocking analysis in the audio callback
evidence_needed: macro-to-action mapping fixtures
observer/audio correlation for each stage gesture
listening review proving each macro changes the room
ideas.promotion_condition: Promote after current destructive gestures and pressure policies are source-aware and human-reviewed.
ideas.release_1_0_boundary: Non-blocking for 1.0; macros must reuse the product spine, not become a second action system.

ideas.title: Taste-aware demo generation
ideas.musical_payoff: Riotbox searches for the best few takes, rejects boring or weak ones, and emits a listening pack with practical reasons.
replay_realtime_risk: automated taste ranking must publish confidence and blind spots
demo candidates must remain human_verdict: unverified until reviewed
evidence_needed: human-label calibration report
boring-output rejection fixtures
demo-bank import path that blocks unverified demo-ready claims
ideas.promotion_condition: Promote only when the audio judge has enough Riotbox-owned labels to rank candidates without pretending to be a hidden oracle.
ideas.release_1_0_boundary: Non-blocking for 1.0; can prepare review packs but cannot grant demo-ready status.

ideas.title: Set-level performance memory
ideas.musical_payoff: Riotbox preserves a set identity across sources while still rebuilding each source into a new aggressive result.
replay_realtime_risk: set memory must not overwrite per-source lineage
cross-song identity must remain replayable across restore and export
evidence_needed: multi-source session replay fixture
set-level listening pack with source-family coverage
metrics showing identity without identical-output collapse
ideas.promotion_condition: Promote after per-source demo readiness is strong enough to define what set identity should preserve.
ideas.release_1_0_boundary: Non-blocking for 1.0; cannot excuse weak per-source sound.

ideas.title: Ecosystem surfaces that preserve sound and QA gates
ideas.musical_payoff: DAW export, stems, controllers, and sync expose Riotbox without weakening the instrument's sound or proof discipline.
replay_realtime_risk: export and sync must preserve action, source, and render identity
controller shortcuts must not bypass queue / commit semantics
external surfaces need the same audio QA and demo evidence boundaries
evidence_needed: stem/export identity fixtures
controller action mapping tests
external-surface PR template requiring sound and QA gate references
ideas.promotion_condition: Promote only after the 10/10 sound and technical QA gates are stable enough to export without hiding weak output.
ideas.release_1_0_boundary: Non-blocking for 1.0 unless a specific surface is explicitly promoted into release scope.
